videos.title: Babble Ride Across Britain 2024
videos.description:
On 7th September, the Ride Across Britain class of 2024 lined up at Land's End to begin their epic journey up the country.

Over the course of the nine day adventure, the RAB peloton traversed mighty mountains, crossed barren moors, zipped down quiet country lanes and wandered through remote valleys. We saw a few perfect weather days and some... not so perfect. In short, we worked our way through 980 miles of the most spectacular scenery Great Britain has to offer.

To every rider, crew member, event partner and supporter who made Ride Across Britain 2024 what it was - thank you. It was truly one to remember.

www.rideacrossbritain.com

videos.description: Land's End to John o'Groats is an iconic ride in British cycle touring, so as part of Team Cycling Plus this year, Reuben Bakker-Dyos enlisted the help of some buddies to ride the end-to-end route in just 6 days.

quotes.text: Here I am at the beautiful Lands End and oh my god you know I'm not even started this ride yet but the views are just incredible like look how beautiful this is it is just stunning.

When you ride this route yourself you realize how extraordinary the end to end record is and just what must be required to achieve this ride in under two days. It's going to take us nine days and we're going to feel that that's pretty tough.

A typical Land End to Jon a groats ride can last anywhere between 9 and 14 days so that's a typical touring route. We decided possibly stupidly to do the ride in 6 days - this averages out about 170 miles a day, with the longest days lasting up to about 15 hours.

key_challenges.name: Derailleur hanger failure
key_challenges.description: Rider experienced a snapped derailleur hanger on the first day, emphasizing the importance of keeping a spare hanger on the bike rather than in camp bags.

key_challenges.name: 17 categorized climbs
key_challenges.description: The route includes 17 counted climbs according to GPS data from riders, with significant elevation changes throughout the journey.

key_challenges.name: Limited food options on Sundays
key_challenges.description: Shops can be closed on Sundays in rural areas, creating potential nutrition challenges during long riding days.

terrain_notes.text: Nice country roads throughout the route with forested areas and rural countryside. Riders describe beautiful rolling terrain with sheep in fields.
Mix of flat and rolling roads where aerodynamic wheels become effective at 40-45 km/h speeds on the flatter sections.

gear_mentions.text: Cannondale Synapse carbon bike recommended for comfort on long distances with 25.4 seatpost for flex and Save Plus micro suspension seat stays for shock absorption.
Enve SES 5.6 disc wheels paired with Victoria Corsa G+ 25mm tires work well for speed on flat sections, though the tires wear out completely after 1000 miles.
Compact chainset 50/34 with 11-32 cassette provides suitable gearing, though one rider switched to 11-28 for smaller gear jumps.

race_day_tips.text: Average 170 miles per day if completing in 6 days, with longest days lasting up to 15 hours, so comfort is crucial for bike setup.
Always keep spare derailleur hanger on the bike rather than in camp bags since mechanical failures happen during riding, not at camp.
Expect progressively colder weather as you head north, with temperatures dropping 1.5-2 degrees each day from south to north.

additional_quotes.text: Take photos at Land's End the night before rather than morning of departure to avoid the queues and rushing on race day.

When you ride this route yourself you realize how extraordinary the end to end record is and just what must be required to achieve this ride in under two days. It's going to take us nine days and we're going to feel that that's pretty tough.

rider_intel.search_text: Ride Across Britain covers 980 miles from Land's End to John O'Groats across varied British terrain. Riders describe nice country roads through rural countryside and forested areas with 17 categorized climbs throughout the route. The challenge typically takes 9-14 days for touring but can be completed in 6 days averaging 170 miles daily with 15-hour riding days. Weather progressively gets colder heading north, dropping 1-2 degrees each day. Mechanical challenges include derailleur hanger failures on rough roads. Limited food options exist on Sundays in rural areas. Aerodynamic equipment becomes effective on flatter sections at 40-45 km/h speeds. Comfort-focused bike setup is crucial for the long daily distances. The route attracts 700-800 participants and showcases beautiful British countryside with sheep-filled fields and rolling terrain.
